amara.name: Dr. Amara Osei

marcus.name: Marcus Odhiambo
marcus.signature: Here's the thing most people don't know...
marcus.signoffLine: Your voice belongs in this conversation. Use it.

teacherLine.text: *Here's what this means for you...* Every year, Kenya's government decides how to spend KES 4.8 trillion — that's the money collected from your taxes, fees, and loans. Most Kenyans never see where it goes. That changes today.

hook.headline: The budget affects every Kenyan — every single day
outcomes: Read any budget document and understand its core structure
Explain the difference between national and county budgets
Identify the 4 major budget documents and when they are released

teacherLine.text: Most people think the budget is one document released on Budget Day. It's not. It's four separate documents, released at different times, each controlling a different part of the money story. Once you know all four, you can track government promises from intention to implementation.

content.sectionTitle: The 4 documents that control Kenya's money

cards.title: Budget Policy Statement
cards.body: Released in February. Sets the overall targets — how much the government plans to spend, borrow, and invest. This is the vision document.
cards.keyPoint: Published by Treasury. Read this first every February.

cards.title: Finance Bill
cards.body: Introduced after the Budget Speech in June. Proposes changes to tax laws — VAT rates, excise duties, income tax brackets. This directly affects your wallet.
cards.keyPoint: This is the bill you can submit comments on. Every Kenyan has that right.

cards.title: Estimates of Revenue & Expenditure
cards.body: The detailed line-by-line breakdown — how much each ministry gets. Tabled in April alongside the Finance Bill.
cards.keyPoint: This is where you find youth fund allocations, health budgets, education figures.

cards.title: Appropriation Act
cards.body: The final approved budget signed into law. Once this is passed, money can legally be released to government entities.
cards.keyPoint: Track whether allocations survive from Estimates to the Appropriation Act.

callout.label: Key Insight
callout.text: A government can promise KES 10B for youth in the BPS — and quietly reduce it to KES 3B in the Estimates. Tracking all four documents exposes this pattern.

quiz.question: The Finance Bill is introduced after the Budget Speech. What is its primary purpose?
options: To report on money already spent by the government
To propose changes to tax laws and revenue collection
To approve county government budgets across all 47 counties
To set the salaries of government officials

feedback.correct: ✓ Exactly right. The Finance Bill proposes new or amended rules for how the government raises money — taxes, levies, and fees. It's the "income rules" bill, not the spending bill.
feedback.wrong: The Finance Bill is forward-looking — it proposes changes to how the government raises revenue. Think of it as the "income rules" document, not the spending one.

teacherLine.text: Understanding the budget cycle isn't just academic. When you know *when* money is supposed to flow from the national government to your county — and to your school, or clinic, or road project — you can ask real questions when it doesn't arrive. That's accountability.

content.sectionTitle: The 6 stages of Kenya's budget year

steps.description: Treasury prepares the Budget Policy Statement (August–January). Macroeconomic targets are set.
Public participation forums open (January–March). Citizens can submit input — this is your first window.
Budget Speech + Finance Bill + Estimates of Revenue tabled in Parliament (June).
Parliamentary committees review, amend, and approve. The Finance Bill goes through public participation here.
Appropriation Act signed. Money is legally approved for release to all government entities.
Ministries and counties begin spending. This is where tracking begins — and where things go wrong.

resources.label: Kenya Law: Finance Bill 2024 full text

quiz.question: A county governor announces a new hospital will be built using "the national government allocation." What document would confirm this allocation exists?
options: The Budget Policy Statement — it outlines all spending intentions
The Estimates of Revenue & Expenditure — the detailed line-by-line budget
The Appropriation Act — which legally approves spending
Both B and C must align before money can legally flow

feedback.correct: ✓ Both matter. The Estimates show the proposed allocation, but the Appropriation Act is what legally allows the money to be released. A line in the Estimates can be cut before the Act is passed.
feedback.wrong: Think about what legally authorises spending. The Estimates propose, the Appropriation Act authorises. Both must show the allocation for it to legally flow to the county.

teacherLine.text: You now have the knowledge. You know the four documents. You know the cycle. You know when your voice is supposed to be heard. The next step — and the power — is yours.

*The data tells a clear story...* Kenya has 48 active budgets — one national budget and one for each of the 47 counties. Most citizens know about Budget Day. But the county budget? That's where your roads, your water, your local health clinic actually gets funded.

hook.headline: The county budget is closer to your life than the national one

statTiles.label: Of national revenue shared to counties (equitable share)

outcomes: Distinguish between national and county budget responsibilities
Identify which level funds which services in your life
Know when and how to participate in your county's budget process

teacherLine.text: The Constitution of Kenya 2010 divided government functions between two levels. This wasn't accidental — it was designed to bring decision-making closer to the people who need services. When your road isn't built, you need to know which government to hold accountable.

content.sectionTitle: The division of functions — national vs county

points.text: **National government** funds: national security, foreign affairs, immigration, major infrastructure (highways, railways, ports), national hospitals
**County governments** fund: local roads, primary healthcare, early childhood education, agriculture extension, local markets
**Devolved functions** include health and education at the primary level — this is why county health budgets matter so much
Some functions are **concurrent** — shared between levels. Water is a classic example: national policy, county implementation
Counties receive an **equitable share** (minimum 15% of revenue, currently ~35%) plus conditional grants and their own locally-generated revenue

callout.label: Watch Out
callout.text: When a county hospital runs out of medicine, the first question is: was the county health budget funded? Was it released? Was it spent correctly? Three separate failure points.

quiz.question: Your county primary school has no textbooks. Which level of government is primarily responsible for funding primary education supplies?
options: The national government through the Ministry of Education
The county government — primary education is a devolved function
The Teachers Service Commission (TSC) which manages all schools
The school board through parent contributions

feedback.correct: ✓ Right. Primary education was devolved to counties under the Constitution. The county education department budget is where textbook allocations should appear — and where you should look when they're missing.
feedback.wrong: Under Kenya's devolved system, primary education is a county function. The county government's education budget is the primary source for school supplies — not the national budget.

teacherLine.text: The numbers don't lie. Your county received money. Your services should exist. When they don't, you now have the vocabulary, the legal grounding, and the process knowledge to ask why — and to be taken seriously when you do.

steps.label: Introduction
steps.title: The Bill That Changes Your Wallet

teacherLine.text: *Here's the thing most people don't know...* Every June, Parliament passes a bill that directly determines how much you pay for cooking oil, mobile data, and basic goods. It's called the Finance Bill — and in 2023, it nearly tripled certain excise duties before public pressure reversed it. Your voice worked then. Let's make sure you're ready for next time.

hook.headline: The Finance Bill determines what things cost in Kenya

statTiles.label: When the Finance Bill is introduced each year
Parliament has to pass it (constitutional limit)
Year Kenyans defeated key Finance Bill clauses

outcomes: Explain what a Finance Bill is and what it can and cannot change
Identify where proposed tax changes are documented in the Bill
Know your rights to submit comments during parliamentary debate
